site.name: EcoScienceHub
site.tagline: Peer-informed writing on ecology, biology, and applied physics
site.description: Independent, scientifically literate writing on ecology, biology, and applied physics. Built to be read slowly and cited carefully.

ecology.label: Ecology
ecology.description: Long-form, peer-informed writing on the systems that sustain life — ecosystems and their services, the climate that shapes them, and the biodiversity that gives them resilience.

biology.label: Biology
biology.description: Cellular, molecular, evolutionary, and developmental biology — the mechanisms that connect a single sequence of bases to the diversity of living form.

physics.label: Applied Physics
physics.description: Applied physics for the climate transition and beyond — energy systems, thermodynamic limits, and the quantum foundations that increasingly matter outside the lab.

ecosystems.label: Ecosystems
ecosystems.description: Forests, wetlands, oceans, soils — how energy and matter move through living systems and what disturbance reveals about them.

climate-change.label: Climate Change
climate-change.description: Drivers, feedbacks, attribution, and the policy-relevant uncertainties at the frontier of climate science.

biodiversity.label: Biodiversity
biodiversity.description: Why species count is the wrong metric, what genetic and functional diversity actually do, and where conservation efforts are working.

cells.label: Cell Biology
cells.description: The cell as the fundamental unit of life — its architecture, energetics, signaling, and the methods used to study it.

genetics.label: Genetics
genetics.description: Molecular genetics, genomics, and the evolution of genetic systems — what sequencing has revealed, what it cannot resolve.

evolution.label: Evolution
evolution.description: Selection, drift, development, and the modern synthesis — including the parts of it that are quietly being revised.

energy.label: Energy
energy.description: Generation, storage, transmission, and the materials and architectures that determine which technologies actually scale.

thermodynamics.label: Thermodynamics
thermodynamics.description: The laws that bound every physical system — and the surprisingly modern questions still open at their frontier.

quantum-basics.label: Quantum Basics
quantum-basics.description: Quantum mechanics for the practitioner — the parts that matter for sensors, materials, and computing, without the philosophy.

home.meta_title: Peer-informed writing on ecology, biology, and applied physics
home.hero_title: Slow, careful writing about the systems that sustain us.
home.hero_lede: EcoScienceHub is a platform for ecology, biology, and applied physics — written by working scientists, edited for clarity, and cited where it matters. Built to be read slowly.
home.mission_title: Science is a slow conversation. We give it the room it needs.
home.mission_p1: Most of what passes for science writing today is optimized for the attention economy. Headlines outrun evidence; nuance is the first thing cut. We started EcoScienceHub because the alternative — careful, cited, accountable writing — is harder to find than it should be.
home.mission_p2: Every article on this platform is organized into a topic hierarchy (Topic → Subtopic → Article), written by a working scientist or experienced science journalist, and kept current with explicit revision dates. When we update something, we say so.
home.topics_title: Three lenses on the natural world

category_hub.subtopics_subtitle: Where the {category} writing on this site is organized.
category_hub.pillars_subtitle: One in-depth foundation article per subtopic — the recommended entry point.
category_hub.latest_subtitle: Most recently updated writing across all subtopics.

subtopic_hub.more_subtitle: All other writing in this subtopic, most recently updated first.
subtopic_hub.empty: No articles published in this subtopic yet — check back soon.

article.fallback_notice: This article has not yet been translated into {language}. The English version is shown below.

insights.title: Arguments worth defending
insights.description: Insights are short, structured essays. Each one stakes a single, falsifiable claim and defends it. They are written to be argued with — clearly, cited, and dated.

discussions.title: Expert-led, moderated conversations
discussions.description: We don't run a forum. We run threads — each one framed by a domain expert, opened to verified specialists, and moderated for substance.
discussions.about_body: Discussions on this site are framed by domain experts and moderated for substance. Verified-expert comments carry a badge; all other submissions are reviewed before they appear.

footer.footer_note: Editorially independent. Citations welcome — please link to the canonical URL.

newsletter.title: One careful piece a week.
newsletter.description: Subscribe to receive new long-form articles and insights, plus occasional notes from the editorial team. No clickbait, no list-buying, no tracking pixels.
